string.text: Unknown mercenary. Well-armed and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Unknown mercenary. Possibly well-armed. No detailed information available.
Leader of the mercenary group. Well-armed and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Unknown mercenary. Well-armed and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
C-Consciousness faction stalker. No detailed information available.
C-Consciousness faction fighter. Well-equipped. No detailed information available.
Mobile scientific camp guard. No detailed information available.
Military commander at the Agroprom Research Institute. Very well armed, protected, and extremely dangerous. Detailed information unavailable.

Former digger. Along with his group of like-minded individuals, he climbs into all the abandoned labs and bunkers he can reach. A seasoned stalker who's been through a lot of scrapes. Will never refuse to help. Generally considered a good guy. But he lost something in the Agroprom area that just won't let him go, something important.

Hasn't been in the Zone long, but settled in nicely. Helps whoever he can - teaches sense to the rookies, helps others too; overall, people think highly of him. Has connections at the Cordon, brings food into the Zone... Things aren't going so well for him on the mainland, but he doesn't spread it around. Stashed away a few food supplies for a rainy day.

Commander of the Duty checkpoint at the Garbage. Righteous guy, keeps his word. He's got plenty of strength... Where he came from and how he signed up with Duty is unknown. Rumor has it he started gathering and hiding stash. Looks like he's planning to bail.

Local street punk. Poses no threat. Apparently, he was a huge movie fan on the mainland, as he likes to imitate the manners of supermen from various action movies. Hence his nickname Die Hard, which people shortened to just Nut. Doesn't keep swag on him - afraid it'll get taken, so he hides everything, somewhere near the Garbage.

A righteous stalker, tries to maintain order in the Zone as he sees it. Sometimes harsh, but fair. Came to the Zone hiding from the cops. And now he chases the local street punks. Though he ain't much better himself. After all, the stash he hid nearby wasn't exactly earned through good deeds either.

A young lieutenant, Kuznetsov's replacement. New to the Zone. Principally doesn't drink. So you don't even need to offer him vodka. Out of youth and stupidity, he willingly takes artifacts. Doesn't trade ammo, although there's a rumor that, like many military guys here, he "wrote off" some munitions and hid them somewhere.
Experienced smuggler. Known for managing to export several hundred mutant mounts from the Exclusion Zone in a short period of time.
Bar guard. Or, if you prefer, Barkeep's bodyguard. Nothing is known about his present, just like his past.
Garik's twin brother. Guard at the bar entrance. Just as silent as his brother.

Neither a saint nor a scoundrel, knows his worth, which earns him respect among stalkers. Rumor has it he's hiding in the Zone from his dark past. After falling into an anomaly, he started limping and decided to change his profession. Set up a bar near the Duty faction, which quietly became the main gathering place for Zone veterans. Barkeep has many connections both inside the Zone and in the outside world.
A constantly drunk member of the Duty faction. Duty gave him one last chance to stay in the faction by completing a mission, but he's drunk and can't do it.

A good-natured, cheerful guy. His only problem is that he only knows how to shoot a hunting rifle. Won't even try anything else. Recently got into a scrape in the Wild Territory and lost his family rifle there. Now hangs around the bar, hoping someone will return his gun to him.
A mysterious stalker who knows a lot. Trades information at the bar.

Former detective, left the cops because he dug up info on some big shot. Immediately joined Duty. All in all, an honest soldier and a smart guy. Knows how to talk to people. Leads all punitive and clearing operations against large mutant concentrations. He's no fool either, doesn't waste time and stores a little something away for a rainy day in a secluded spot.
The Arena master's bodyguard. Only bad things are known about his past, hates his own brothers.
One of the founders of Duty and its main ideologist. He developed the rulebook and recruited almost all of Duty's key members.
Head of security for the Duty territory.
Unknown person affected by the Brain Scorcher.
Founder of the Duty faction, has been leading the faction for five years now.
Unknown stalker. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Unknown stalker. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
One of the bandit leaders. Well-armed and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Unknown stalker. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Re-enlisted Captain of the Ukrainian Armed Forces. No detailed information available.

Became the leader of the rookie village after Wolf. Driven, aggressive, enjoys well-deserved authority among the rookies. He has some kind of urge to command others, but if he senses someone has more authority, he stays in the shadows, doesn't stick his neck out much. But if there's no one "equal" around, his leadership streak kicks right in. In short, give a guy like him an inch and he'll take command. This commanding nature helped him quickly gather stash, which he hid nearby.

An old, hardened loner of the Zone. Very few know Fox closely. He's bros with Redhead and Mole. They make runs together, gather swag together. Doesn't really talk to anyone - a "hello" and they part ways. Why he keeps to himself, nobody knows, and nobody really looked into it, but it's assumed Fox has some special interest in the Zone, not just a material one. Lately, he's also been seen hanging around with the Doctor. Word is he's got a stash with artifacts... but then again, they say that about everyone.

A sharp rookie. Though he hasn't been in the Zone long, he's shown himself to be a decent guy. Made a couple of runs deep into the Zone, and although he didn't bring back huge hauls, he didn't come back empty-handed either. Doesn't go looking for trouble, but doesn't hide behind his comrades' backs. Enjoys Wolf's special favor, who often sends him on important tasks. Served somewhere down south, so he had to participate in real combat a couple of times; he knows which end of a Kalash shoots and what mutual support in battle means. Overall, he's one of those you can rely on in a tight spot. But he's no fool either - he's got a stash hidden away somewhere.

A legendary figure in his own right, they say he can guide you to any point in the Zone. He charges a lot, but it's not just about the money. He won't guide people he doesn't like for any amount of cash. A veteran, knows the Zone inside and out.
Conscript Sergeant of the Ukrainian Armed Forces. No detailed information available.

A stalker nicknamed Nimble for his ability to be unnoticeable and indispensable. The trader sometimes uses him for delicate tasks: tailing someone, taking a peek, eavesdropping. Such useful people always keep to themselves and stay independent, even though they completely rely on their "employer". You can hardly consider him a real stalker, as he simply has no time for stalking; he's always busy with errands and tasks. But he's a useful man. Just like everyone else, he's got some hidden stash.

Major, commands the army checkpoint at the bridge. Cooperates with stalkers, taking a bribe for passage across the bridge. Stalkers consider him their own kind of guy. He's a soldier, of course, and if you try to force your way through, he'll order you shot with a clear conscience, but he has a weakness for the national beverage - vodka. He'll let you pass for a bottle, and for a few more, he'll even toss some gear your way. Been hanging around here for a long time, has no family, so he doesn't take leaves; you could say he's a local landmark. Someone said he asked for this duty himself, to be farther from the brass and closer to an opportunity to make some cash... He's got a stash of "written-off" ammo somewhere.
Conscript Private of the Ukrainian Armed Forces. No detailed information available.
Unknown mercenary. Well-armed and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Rookie. New to the Zone. No detailed information available.

Just a stalker. Rookie. Keeps trying to act tough, but as soon as things get real, he goes weak in the knees. He might seem reliable to some, but it's best not to do serious business with him. Came to the Zone recently, thought he'd quickly make some cash and bail; someone fed him fairy tales about how easy and fast it is to get swag. But it didn't happen fast, and he doesn't have the guts to go on a solo raid for loot. So he just loiters at the Cordon for now. Quite the hustler! Quietly swipes things from other stalkers and moves them to his own stashes.

Leader of the rookie camp. An old stalker who was about to retire. Right now he runs the camp, teaches the young blood, protects them. Wandered the Zone plenty, got more than enough experience. Rumor has it he stashed a fat pile of loot somewhere nearby. Some even tried to squeeze him on the subject, but he stays quiet as a fish. Considering he's an experienced stalker who's been in the Zone a long time, one has to assume his stash is pretty decent. Meaning he's already secured a comfortable life for himself on the mainland... if, of course, he lives to see that happy day when he actually leaves the Zone and retires.

A blood deficiency that led the poor guy to plasma protein deficiency and decreased blood osmotic pressure also influenced his grim nickname. An incredibly lucky stalker. Many times his group was a hair's breadth from death, and he was always the only one who managed to survive. He has a bad reputation among stalkers as an unlucky charm. He's been flying solo for a while now. The general opinion is that it's better not to mess with him for the sake of your health. It's surprising that with such a reputation among stalkers, he's still alive... Truly a lucky guy. And all because he's got a stash of meds somewhere.

Sidorovich's guard. Although rumor has it they have a much closer relationship. Used to be considered a lucky stalker, but lately prefers to keep his head down; maybe stepped on someone's toes, or something else... Now he hangs out as Sidor's guard. Knows where to get vodka, but doesn't sell it, instead offering to roll some dice out of boredom. Some people even manage to win against him. Plays for money and artifacts, which he most likely unloads to that same Sidor, who probably supplies him with the vodka. Nobody's really figured out the scheme of their joint operation yet, but one thing is for sure: Stas ain't losing out. Why he's hoarding money remains a question... and he stubbornly refuses to answer it. Carries vodka from somewhere, either the Swamps or the Gut.

Made a living trading since he was a kid. Sold colored pencils in kindergarten. Traded chewing gum in school. Dealt in light drugs at the institute. With the appearance of the Zone, he figured out you could make good money off it. Known for being able to establish good trading relations with the most diverse clients. Stalkers believe it's best not to cross him, or you won't even be able to hide outside the Zone. How he manages to constantly restock his supplies and assortment while sitting in one place remains a mystery, because even when he disappears for a couple of days, there are no wagon trains of goods following him when he returns. Most likely, he runs all his business through the military, who deliver whatever he orders. What he pays them with is known only to him. They say he's got a large warehouse of provisions somewhere - for long-term storage.
Enjoys a certain amount of authority among the bandits in the Zone. Borov's accomplice.
Duty faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Duty faction officer. No detailed information available.
Rookie. Taking his first steps in the Zone.
Fled to the Zone, hiding from law enforcement. Tries to maintain order in the Zone alone, as he sees it. A fighter against lawlessness. Harsh, but fair.
Rookie. New to the Zone. No detailed information available.
A stalker with a dirty face and an equally dirty biography.
Experienced stalker. No detailed information available.
Duty faction fighter. Usually well-equipped. No detailed information available.

Commander of a Duty special forces squad. Usually well-equipped. Former SWAT, principled. One problem - he's used to solving all issues only by force, straight as a post, no flexibility. Came to the Zone along with his former comrades-in-arms. Most of them stick together with him. Carried out several operations for Duty, the leadership values him. Loiters at the Warehouses all the time - rumor has it, guarding his stash.

Self-taught technician for guns and armor. Fixed a gun for one guy, patched up armor for another - and the guys respected him. Barkeep immediately took him under his wing. If there's a serious breakdown, you can take it to him - he'll restore it. Doesn't deal with weapons right now, but will fix any armor. Has a stash of repair kits and tools nearby.

Electronics specialist. Barkeep ordered him from the mainland from some research institute. Pays him money, guards him like the apple of his eye. If you have any problems with a PDA, go straight to him. If you need to find someone in the Zone - him too. Even though he ain't a stalker himself, he does hide artifacts. And not too far away.

It's unclear where he came from, but he knows an awful lot. Usually he can be found in the bar; he trades info there, buys blueprints, documents, and records from the labs. Who he's connected with is unknown, but people are wary of him. In a word, a shady character. They say he got tight with Sultan, so he's definitely got something stashed away, maybe arts, or maybe even a unique item.
Duty faction fighter. Usually well-equipped. No detailed information available.
An informer and snitch known throughout the Zone.
Unknown mercenary. Well-armed and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Unknown mercenary. Judging by everything, new to the Zone. No detailed information available.
Unknown mercenary. Possibly well-armed. No detailed information available.
One of the Zone's veterans. This stalker knows the Zone like the back of his hand. Usually well-equipped and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Veteran stalker. Possibly well-equipped. No detailed information available.
Freedom faction stalker. Commander of the Barrier. A man who gathered people around him ready to die for a common cause.
Guard for "Lukash", leader of the Freedom faction.
Freedom faction cook. A drunk and a brawler, but cooks excellently.
A mechanic known in the Zone. Can build a tank out of a tractor.
Freedom faction master. No detailed information available.
Keeper of the Freedom faction. Fanatically loyal to the faction. Excellently equipped and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Keeper of the Freedom faction. Excellently equipped and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Freedom faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Freedom faction sniper. No detailed information available.
Freedom faction fighter. Usually well-equipped. No detailed information available.
Known for his greed and stubbornness, but they say you can get anything from him.
Freedom faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Freedom faction fighter. Usually well-equipped. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Elite Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Leader of the Monolith faction. No detailed information available.
Rank-and-file Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction special forces stalker. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction fighter. Well-equipped. No detailed information available.
Army special forces. Well-equipped. Detailed information unavailable.
One of the Zone's veterans. This stalker knows the Zone like the back of his hand. Usually well-equipped and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.
Veteran stalker. Possibly well-equipped. No detailed information available.
Freedom faction master. No detailed information available.
One of the stalkers who got unlucky.
Mobile scientific laboratory employee. No detailed information available.
Scientist, expert in anomalous phenomena. Worked in America on secret projects. Recently considered missing in action.

Marauder and murderer. A bounty has been put on his head multiple times. Used to be on good terms with Duty, helped them capture their current territory. Now dwells in the Wild Territory and robs lone stalkers with his gang.
Doctor of Biological Sciences. Outside the Zone, he studied pathogenic mutations in animals. Went to the Zone to study mutations in blind dogs.
One of the "heroes" of the Mercenaries faction. A veteran shot through by all calibers. A rare psycho and sadist.
One of the Mercenaries' field commanders. Commander of a squad of fighters who call themselves "dogs". An anarchist, despises all laws except the laws of force.
Psycho-material image created by the Monolith.
Unknown stalker. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Unknown stalker. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.

Came to the Zone looking for adventure. Tries to act like a 60s comic book hero. They call him differently: Batman, Macho, Darkwing, Banderas... and although the fact that he's been in the Zone for two years and is still alive speaks volumes, they still don't take him seriously, which really hurts his feelings.

Fierce, angry, rude, selfish, cruel, callous, cold. Nobody knows when or how he got into the Zone. People fear and dislike him. He just lives in the Zone; he has neither a future nor a past. After one of his many runs into the Zone, he lost his memory and forgot a lot. Doesn't remember where he's from, doesn't remember his real name, suffers from frequent memory lapses. For example, he might forget a new acquaintance an hour after meeting them and, perceiving them as an enemy, kill them.
Rookie bandit. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Unknown stalker. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Keeper of the Duty faction. If you're not his friend, you'd better avoid him. Detailed information unavailable.
Duty faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Duty faction fighter. Usually well-equipped. No detailed information available.

A boor, a sycophant, a hypocrite, a coward. Barin's life credo could be called the phrase "after us - the deluge." Tries to profit from everything. Despite all this, he is very ambitious and touchy. Came to the Zone, as clich? as it sounds, for money. And now, for the second year in the Zone, he's been extracting material wealth by any means necessary.
Duty faction fighter. Usually well-equipped. No detailed information available.

This is an official SSU recruiter. Often sits in the bar. Everyone knows about this, as well as the fact that if things get too tight, you should go to him. However, after this step, the way back to honest stalkers will be closed.
Unknown mercenary. Possibly well-armed. No detailed information available.
Unknown mercenary. Possibly well-armed. No detailed information available.
Unknown mercenary. Possibly well-armed. No detailed information available.
Unknown mercenary. Possibly well-armed. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Military stalker. Well-armed and extremely dangerous. Detailed information unavailable.
Rank-and-file soldier. No detailed information available.
Army special forces. Well-equipped. Detailed information unavailable.
Experienced stalker. No detailed information available.

An incredibly lucky stalker. Many times his group was a hair's breadth from death, and he was always the only one who managed to survive. He has a bad reputation among stalkers as an "unlucky charm." He's been flying solo for a while now.
One of the Zone's veterans. This stalker knows the Zone like the back of his hand. Usually well-equipped and extremely dangerous. No detailed information available.

Experienced smuggler. Hunted in Africa and South America for a long time. The stories about Chernobyl mutants that flew around the world brought him to Ukraine. In a short period of time, he managed to export several hundred mutant mounts from the exclusion zone, for which he received the nickname "Chuchelo". In between "work" in the Zone, he's a successful trader. Loves Armani suits and Versace ties, but prefers Soviet guns.

Former Afghan war vet. During a Mujahideen ambush, he suddenly found faith in God. After Afghan, he immigrated to Poland, where he entered a theological seminary. After graduating from the seminary, he preached for some time in the Lviv Catholic Church. After the explosion at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant, he volunteered to help the victims. He has been preaching in the Zone for the last few years.

In the past, a mechanic at the KholodMash plant. After his wife left him, he decided to commit suicide, and since he was a religious man, he chose the Zone as an original method of suicide. But he survived, got used to it over a couple of years, and gained a certain amount of authority among stalkers thanks to his professional skills. At the moment, Master is the most competent mechanic in the Zone; repairing equipment and machinery, setting up protection systems for faction bunkers, etc., etc. is his domain. Has access to any door, influential, simple but rough in communication. Hides everything he gets or finds. Rumor has it he's got a rich stash, if you can find it, of course.
Rookie. New to the Zone. No detailed information available.

He had nothing and no one left on the mainland, so he decided to come here. He simply has nowhere to go if he leaves the Zone. He barely makes ends meet in the Zone. Owes some amounts of money to very many stalkers. Goes into the Zone very often, but never alone; usually tags along with groups of stalkers. But few agree to take him, because he's extra baggage. Rarely gathers swag. Lives on handouts from other stalkers and is therefore up to his neck in debt. But like everyone else, he hides something in stashes, probably some junk.
Veteran stalker. Possibly well-equipped. No detailed information available.
Veteran stalker. Possibly well-equipped. No detailed information available.

Gusar. A former "black beret", served in the Bila Tserkva special forces brigade. Re-enlisted senior sergeant, sniper and demolitions expert by specialty. A "lone wolf" at heart. Never takes partners with him into the Zone, preferring to act alone. He has repeatedly been offered to join clans - he always flatly refused for this exact reason.

Physics student, left his fifth year of university for the Zone to make money. Loves to show off his "scientific" knowledge and discuss abstract philosophical topics. Stalkers dislike him for this showing off. But overall, he's not a bad guy. Still believes in high ideals.

Cunning, far-sighted, but sometimes makes gross mistakes. Despicable at times. Overall, leaves a mixed impression. Thin and pale. Came to the Zone out of curiosity and promised himself: two months in the Zone, then home. But he stayed here. On his first run into the Zone, he managed to walk without detectors across a field entirely covered with anomalies, not knowing about them. When he was told about this, it took him a long time to recover. Since then, he celebrates that day (October 9) as his second birthday. He has some unhealthy addiction to energy drinks. So he hides them everywhere - here and there.
Freedom faction master. No detailed information available.
Freedom faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Freedom faction fighter. Usually well-equipped. No detailed information available.

Leshy, as his brother nicknamed him, came to the Zone as a young idealist. But a few years of living a hair's breadth from death made him a cynical realist. Considers the Zone his home, and those who haven't passed the Zone's test - unworthy to live on Earth.
Writes essays and stories about the Zone under the pseudonym Arkady. Formerly a journalist; meticulous, tough, but fair. Enjoys a certain amount of respect among stalkers.
One of the stalkers who got unlucky.
One of the stalkers who got unlucky.
One of the stalkers who got unlucky.
Bandit guard. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Rank-and-file bandit. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Rookie bandit. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Experienced bandit. Shady reputation. No detailed information available.
Duty faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Monolith faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Duty faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Duty faction stalker. No detailed information available.
Rank-and-file soldier. No detailed information available.
Army special forces. Well-equipped. Detailed information unavailable.
Experienced stalker. No detailed information available.
Rookie. New to the Zone. No detailed information available.
Veteran of Zone research. Participated in the first expeditions of 2007.
Scientific worker of the mobile laboratory. No detailed information available.
